title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home! Our easy recipe guides you step-by-step for perfect results every time.
lowCompetitionKeywords: Haemul Pajeon with squid and shrimp
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon: Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Haemul Pajeon
Tips for a Crispy Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, an authentic Korean seafood pancake with green onions and various seafood.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a freshly made, crispy Haemul Pajeon, a beloved Korean seafood pancake perfect for any meal or snack.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, generously loaded with a delightful medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Its crispy edges and tender, flavorful interior make it an irresistible dish, perfect as an appetizer or a light meal. Served with a tangy dipping sauce, it offers a harmonious blend of textures and tastes that truly captures the essence of Korean comfort food.

ingredients.notes: about 10-12 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch pieces
peeled, deveined, chopped into bite-sized pieces
cleaned, sliced into rings or bite-sized pieces
thinly sliced, for garnish and slight heat
Korean chili flakes, for dipping sauce

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and prepare your seafood. Chop shrimp and squid into bite-sized pieces. If using oysters or mussels, ensure they are clean. Wash and trim green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili pepper.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, cold water, and the beaten egg until a smooth batter forms. Do not overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable. The batter should be thick enough to coat the ingredients but still pourable.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Gently fold the prepared seafood and sliced yellow onion into the batter. Reserve the green onions and red chili for assembly.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan. The pan should be hot enough for the batter to sizzle immediately.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Pour about 1/4 of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin, round pancake. Immediately arrange a generous layer of green onion pieces on top of the batter, pressing them down slightly. Scatter a few slices of red chili pepper. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges, then carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 4-5 minutes on the second side until golden and crispy.

instructions.title: Repeat and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the cooked Haemul Pajeon from the pan and place it on a cutting board or serving plate. Repeat the cooking process with the remaining batter and ingredients, adding oil as needed for each pancake. While the pancakes cook, prepare the dipping sauce by combining soy sauce, rice vinegar, gochugaru, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds in a small bowl. Serve the hot, crispy pancakes immediately with the dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a combination of all-purpose flour and rice flour in your batter. Adding a touch of cornstarch also helps achieve that perfect crunch.
Ensure your pan is very hot before pouring the batter. This is key to getting a beautifully crispy exterior without overcooking the seafood.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking. The crispiness is best when fresh, though leftovers can be reheated in a dry pan or air fryer to restore some crunch.
article:
Discover the ultimate Korean comfort food that tantalizes taste buds with its crispy edges and savory, seafood-packed interior: Haemul Pajeon. This beloved Korean seafood pancake is more than just a dish; it's an experience, often enjoyed with friends and family, especially when paired with a refreshing glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) on a rainy day. If you've ever craved a dish that combines the fresh flavors of the ocean with the satisfying crunch of a perfectly fried pancake, then Haemul Pajeon is your next culinary adventure.

## What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?

At its heart, Haemul Pajeon is a savory Korean pancake generously studded with a variety of seafood and an abundance of green onions (scallions). The name itself tells its story: "Haemul" translates to seafood, "Pa" refers to green onion, and "Jeon" signifies a pan-fried dish or pancake. Unlike sweet pancakes typically enjoyed for breakfast, Haemul Pajeon is a distinctly savory affair, served as an appetizer, a side dish, or even a light meal.

What makes this dish so irresistible is the textural contrast it offers. The exterior boasts a delightful crispiness, often achieved through a combination of flours and careful frying, while the interior remains soft and moist, cradling succulent pieces of shrimp, squid, and other seafood, all brightened by the earthy sweetness of green onions. It’s a dish that embodies both simplicity and sophistication, delivering a burst of umami with every bite.

## Ingredients for Perfect Haemul Pajeon

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon starts with selecting fresh, high-quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need to gather:

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/2 cup rice flour (for extra crispiness)
*   1/4 cup cornstarch (optional, for added crunch)
*   1 large egg
*   1 1/2 cups ice-cold water (or very cold water)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of white pepper

For the Fillings:
*   2 bunches green onions (about 15-20 stalks), trimmed and cut into 3-4 inch lengths
*   1/2 medium onion, thinly sliced
*   1/2 cup shrimp, peeled, deveined, and chopped into bite-sized pieces
*   1/2 cup squid, cleaned and thinly sliced into rings or strips
*   1/4 cup mussels or oysters (optional, cleaned)
*   1 small red chili, thinly sliced (optional, for a touch of heat)
*   Vegetable oil or other neutral oil for frying

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)
*   1 clove garlic, minced

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Haemul Pajeon

Making Haemul Pajeon at home is surprisingly straightforward and incredibly rewarding. Follow these steps for a delicious result:

1.  **Prepare the Seafood:** If using frozen seafood, thaw it thoroughly. Pat all seafood very dry with paper towels. This is crucial to prevent a watery batter. Chop larger pieces into bite-sized portions.
2.  **Prepare the Vegetables:** Wash and trim the green onions. Cut them into 3-4 inch long pieces. Thinly slice the onion and red chili (if using).
3.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch (if using), salt, and white pepper. In a separate bowl, lightly beat the egg with the ice-cold water. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking until just combined. A few lumps are okay; do not overmix, as this can lead to a tough pancake. Let the batter rest for 10-15 minutes.
4.  **Combine Fillings:** Gently fold the prepared seafood and sliced onion into the batter. Reserve about a third of the green onion pieces for arranging on top later.
5.  **Cook the Pancake:** Heat a large, non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring the entire bottom is coated. When the oil shimmers, pour about 1 cup of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. Immediately arrange some of the reserved green onion pieces on top of the batter in a visually appealing pattern.
6.  **Fry to Perfection:** Cook for 3-5 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges before carefully flipping the pancake. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side, pressing down gently with your spatula occasionally to ensure even cooking and crispiness. The Haemul Pajeon should be cooked through and golden brown on both sides.
7.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked pancake to a cutting board. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Slice the Haemul Pajeon into wedges or squares and serve immediately.
8.  **Make the Dipping Sauce:** While the pancakes are cooking, combine all dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl and stir well.

## Tips for a Crispy Haemul Pajeon

The hallmark of an excellent Haemul Pajeon is its irresistible crispiness. Here are some pro tips to achieve that perfect crunch:

*   **Use Cold Water:** Ice-cold water in the batter helps inhibit gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crisper pancake.
*   **Rice Flour and Cornstarch are Key:** Don't skip the rice flour; it's essential for that desired crisp texture. Cornstarch can further enhance this.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** Overmixing develops gluten, which makes pancakes chewy, not crispy. A slightly lumpy batter is fine.
*   **Plenty of Hot Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil. A generous amount helps "fry" the pancake rather than just cook it, leading to crispier edges. Ensure the oil is hot before adding the batter.
*   **Even Thickness:** Spread the batter evenly in the pan. A consistent thickness ensures even cooking and crispiness throughout.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Like most fried foods, Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan when it's at its crispiest.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile. It shines as an appetizer, a satisfying snack, or a delightful side dish to a larger Korean meal.

The accompanying dipping sauce is a crucial element that balances the richness of the pancake. A simple mix of soy sauce, rice vinegar, a dash of sesame oil, and a sprinkle of sesame seeds creates a tangy, savory, and slightly nutty complement. For those who enjoy a little heat, a pinch of gochugaru or a finely minced garlic clove can be added to the sauce.

Traditionally, Haemul Pajeon is famously paired with makgeolli, a milky, lightly carbonated Korean rice wine. The sweet and tangy notes of makgeolli cut through the savory richness of the pancake beautifully. It also pairs well with soju, beer, or a refreshing iced tea. Serve it alongside kimchi for an authentic Korean dining experience.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a crowd-pleaser, there are many ways to customize it to your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Omit the seafood and load up on vegetables like mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), zucchini, bell peppers, carrots, or even crumbled firm tofu.
*   **Meat Lovers:** Incorporate thinly sliced pork belly, beef, or even kimchi for a hearty Kimchi Pajeon.
*   **Different Seafood:** Feel free to mix and match your favorite seafood. Scallops, small clams, or even imitation crab can be excellent additions.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Substitute the all-purpose flour with a gluten-free flour blend to make a gluten-free version. Ensure your rice flour is also certified gluten-free.
*   **Spicier Kick:** Increase the amount of red chili in the batter or add more gochugaru to the dipping sauce for an extra fiery pancake.
*   **Cheesy Twist:** For a modern fusion, sprinkle some mozzarella cheese over the Haemul Pajeon during the last few minutes of cooking for a gooey, cheesy layer.

Whether you’re a seasoned Korean food enthusiast or new to its vibrant flavors, making Haemul Pajeon at home is a rewarding culinary journey. Its delightful combination of textures and flavors makes it a standout dish that’s sure to impress. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your skillet, and prepare to enjoy a truly authentic and delicious Korean seafood pancake experience!
